summary: Full backend + frontend testing of StockPulse India app. All 14 pytest backend tests PASSED (health, market-status, search, watchlist CRUD, duplicate 409, PATCH reset, settings validation, alert fire/reset with mocked Twilio, cascade delete). Frontend dashboard loads with correct title, market status pill (MARKET CLOSED/SUN since today is Sunday), IST clock, MOCK banner, functional stock search dropdown, AddAlertDialog with all 3 alert types, watchlist table with mono price + BELOW badge + ARMED pill + edit/delete actions, Refresh button and WhatsApp number save flow. No console errors detected. yfinance worked (RELIANCE price Rs.1,430.8 fetched live).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py cron trigger uses hour='9-15' minute='*/5' which actually fires at 9:00-15:55 (not strictly 9:15-15:30). The is_market_open() guard inside scheduled_job() correctly filters, so functionally OK, but cron expression is loose.
WatchlistUpdate.model_dump() without exclude_unset=True treats explicit None as 'no update' but also means a user cannot clear a field - acceptable here since only alert_type/threshold are updatable.
fetch_price uses blocking yf.Ticker().history() inside async handler - not truly async; under load will block the event loop. Consider asyncio.to_thread(). Not blocking functionality.
nse_symbols.py has duplicate 'CONCOR' entry; harmless due to seen-set dedup.
No rate-limiting on /api/search or /api/watchlist/refresh; fine for single-user scope.
seed_data_creation: Tests create & cleanup RELIANCE.NS/TCS.NS watchlist entries. Settings destination set to +919999999999.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_backend.py (pytest). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Uses public BASE_URL market-pulse-in-17.preview.emergentagent.com. Twilio is in MOCK mode (delivery_status='mocked'). Today was Sunday so market pill shows CLOSED - retest on weekday to see green 'Market Open' pulse. yfinance is flaky in rate-limit scenarios; tests have a skip guard for that.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All requested functionality working end-to-end.
